heading: Definitions
label: 2401 Definitions
notes: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–206, § 1134 Stat. 998Dec. 4, 2020“This Act [enacting this chapter and amending , Crimes and Criminal Procedure] may be cited as the ‘Rodchenkov Anti-Doping Act of 2019’.”section 3663A of Title 18
amendments: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–206, § 1134 Stat. 998Dec. 4, 2020“This Act [enacting this chapter and amending , Crimes and Criminal Procedure] may be cited as the ‘Rodchenkov Anti-Doping Act of 2019’.”section 3663A of Title 18

children.heading: Anti-doping organization
children.label: 1 Anti-doping organization
children.text: The term “anti-doping organization” has the meaning given the term in Article 2 of the Convention.

children.heading: Athlete
children.label: 2 Athlete
children.text: The term “athlete” has the meaning given the term in Article 2 of the Convention.

children.heading: Code
children.label: 3 Code
children.text: The term “Code” means the World Anti-Doping Code most recently adopted by WADA on .March 5, 2003

children.heading: Convention
children.label: 4 Convention
children.text: The term “Convention” means the United Nations Educational, Scientific, and Cultural Organization International Convention Against Doping in Sport done at Paris , and ratified by the United States in 2008.October 19, 2005

children.label: i
children.text: in which one or more United States athletes and three or more athletes from other countries participate;

children.label: ii
children.text: that is governed by the anti-doping rules and principles of the Code; and

children.label: I
children.text: the competition organizer or sanctioning body receives sponsorship or other financial support from an organization doing business in the United States; or

children.label: II
children.text: the competition organizer or sanctioning body receives compensation for the right to broadcast the competition in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: includes a competition that is a single event or a competition that consists of a series of events held at different times which, when combined, qualify an athlete or team for an award or other recognition.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 6 Person
children.text: The term “person” means any individual, partnership, corporation, association, or other entity.

children.heading: Prohibited method
children.label: 7 Prohibited method
children.text: The term “prohibited method” has the meaning given the term in Article 2 of the Convention.

children.heading: Prohibited substance
children.label: 8 Prohibited substance
children.text: The term “prohibited substance” has the meaning given the term in Article 2 of the Convention.

children.heading: Scheme in commerce
children.label: 9 Scheme in commerce
children.text: The term “scheme in commerce” means any scheme effectuated in whole or in part through the use in interstate or foreign commerce of any facility for transportation or communication.

children.heading: USADA
children.label: 10 USADA
children.text: The term “USADA” means the United States Anti-Doping Agency.

children.heading: WADA
children.label: 11 WADA
children.text: The term “WADA” means the World Anti-Doping Agency.
